note: No split (factor 1) -> nominal = raw close; modern feeds show the correct contemporaneous Class-A close. DELTA EXPLANATION (~0.77%): the card as_of_price $175.44 is the StatMuse Class-A historical close (S005, two independent StatMuse queries agreed); Yahoo's GOOGL feed and the independent stockanalysis.com feed both report $176.79 for the same Class-A 2024-06-14 close. So the 0.768% gap is a CLOSE-SOURCE difference (StatMuse vs Yahoo/stockanalysis), NOT a share-class swap -- Yahoo GOOG (Class C) closed higher at $178.37, which the card raw_extracts explicitly flags and refuses to use. The card's $175.44 reconciles EXACTLY with the reported market cap (12,381M shares x $175.44 = $2.1721T = card), confirming it is the intended Class-A nominal. Both candidate closes are <1% apart, so the cross-check PASSes. Card NOT changed.

live_fields.low_high_hug_reason: Near-52wk-high entry that is LEGITIMATE, not a series-max grab. $175.44 is -2.75% off the trailing-52wk high ($180.41) -- inside the 3% band -- but this is the genuine verified last close: GOOGL was trading near its highs in mid-June 2024 after the strong Q1'24 beat (+15% revenue, op margin 32%), and the $175.44/$176.79 level is independently triple-confirmed (StatMuse Class-A close, Yahoo/stockanalysis $176.79, and mktcap-implied 12,381M x $175.44 = card $2.1721T). It is the last close <= as_of, not the 52wk-high value accidentally pulled from the series. +52% above the 52wk low ($115.35). Analogous to the NVDA exemplar's justified near-high entry.

live_fields.value: As of 2024-06-14: DOJ v. Google (search-monopoly, Judge Amit Mehta) is PENDING -- the bench trial that began Sept 2023 heard closing arguments May 2-3, 2024; NO ruling had been issued by the as-of date. DOJ alleged ~90% general-search share. The eventual Aug-2024 liability ruling and the remedies phase are AFTER as_of and must NOT be used.

sources.note: closing arguments May 2-3 2024; case pending, no ruling by as_of

live_fields.value: As of 2024-06-14: Alphabet gives no formal forward revenue guidance. The live as-of-state capital signals are the Q1'24 print (S001, 2024-04-25): first-ever quarterly dividend ($0.20/sh) + a new $70B buyback authorization, and a capex ramp of $12.0B in Q1'24 (+91% YoY) with no forward capex guidance -- the AI-infra spend curve that is the binding watch-item.

sources.note: Q1'24 capex $12.0B (+91% YoY); first dividend + $70B buyback announced
